char.name: Resh
char.bio: Herdsman in the camp of Abram. We follow the man and his God across this land, tent to tent, well to well. He treats his servants well, and the flocks are fat this season. Praise be.

friends.name: Eliezer of Damascus
friends.bio: Steward of Abraham's house. If it must be trusted, trust it to me. Off to find a wife for the young master.

friends.name: Lirit
friends.bio: Midwife to the women of Abraham's camp. When old Sarah laughed at the very thought of a baby, well, I've delivered a few 'impossible' ones in my time. Send for me when your time comes, and don't be afraid. 🤍

friends.name: Mavah
friends.bio: Weaver and tentmaker in Abraham's camp. New goat-hair panels woven for the big tent before the cold sets in. Slow work, good work. A strong tent is a happy household.

friends.name: Abram
friends.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

friends.name: Sarai
friends.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

friends.name: Lot
friends.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

friends.name: Isaac
friends.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

friends.name: Rebekah
friends.bio: Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨

friends.name: Esau
friends.bio: Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹

friends.name: Jacob
friends.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

friends.name: Leah
friends.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

friends.name: Rachel
friends.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍

posts.author: CHAR_667
posts.body: I do not understand what I am watching. We walked three days barely speaking. This morning the master looked up, saw the mountain, and told Eliezer and me to stay here with the donkey. Then he said something I cannot get out of my head. He said: the boy and I will go over there and worship, and then WE will come back to you. Both of them. Come back. But he loaded the wood onto Isaac's shoulders, and he is carrying the fire and the knife himself, and there is no lamb. Three days on the road, and no lamb. I have never been so afraid of a thing I do not understand.
posts.highlight: 'we will come back to you'; Isaac carries the wood; there is no lamb (Gen 22:4-6)
posts.ember: Abraham's faith that God will raise or restore the boy (Heb 11:19); the son carries the wood up the hill

author_info.name: Resh
author_info.bio: Herdsman in the camp of Abram. We follow the man and his God across this land, tent to tent, well to well. He treats his servants well, and the flocks are fat this season. Praise be.

posts.author: CHAR_667
posts.body: I have herded goats my whole life. Last night I was a soldier. We caught them near Dan in the dark, split into companies the way the master laid it out, and fell on them from every side at once. It was chaos and terror and over faster than I can tell it. We chased them half the night and we got everyone back. Lot. The women. The children. Every last cart of stolen goods. Three hundred and eighteen shepherds against the armies of four kings, and we are riding home in the sunrise. Do not tell me God is not with that man.
posts.react_note: Mavah, Hodael the Elder and many others

comments.author: CHAR_677
comments.body: My husband left a herdsman and came home a war hero and he has NOT stopped talking about it. Someone please take the sword away before he hurts himself with it.

author_info.name: Mavah
author_info.bio: Weaver and tentmaker in Abraham's camp. New goat-hair panels woven for the big tent before the cold sets in. Slow work, good work. A strong tent is a happy household.

comments.author: CHAR_667
comments.body: @CHAR_677 I will have you know I was very brave and only slightly lost for most of it.

posts.author: CHAR_667
posts.body: So here is my week. My master @CHAR_049 announces that God has told him to pack up the whole household, the flocks, the herds, the tents, all of us, and walk to a land he cannot name because he has never seen it and does not know where it is. My wife says he has finally lost his mind. I told her the man is seventy-five and has never once steered us wrong, and if God was going to speak to anybody in these parts it was always going to be him. So we are going. I have no idea where. I will let you all know if we ever arrive. 🐐
posts.react_note: Mavah, Hodael the Elder and many others

comments.author: CHAR_677
comments.body: Rolled up my entire loom for this. If we are walking to nowhere, I am at least walking there in good company. Save me a spot by the fire.
